title: [CD P1] Gate Docker publishing on required CI and remove duplicate frontend validation
description:
## Summary

Make app-image publication depend on the exact commit's required CI results, then remove the duplicate frontend and generated-contract validation currently rerun inside `.github/workflows/docker-publish.yml`.

Depends on #3327. It should also land after the required backend summary work in #3326 so the release gate can rely on stable, lightweight check contexts.

## Problem

The app-image workflow currently triggers independently on pushes to `main`. Its AMD64 leg installs Python and Node dependencies and reruns:

```bash
npm run ui:typecheck
npm run ui:lint
npm run ui:test
npm run contracts:check
```

These checks duplicate work already owned by the main CI workflow. However, simply deleting them would be unsafe while Docker publication can start independently of the exact commit's required CI results.

The release path must first establish an explicit, auditable dependency on the exact commit's successful checks. Only then should the image workflow become a build, image-verification, and publication pipeline rather than a second test pipeline.

## Required release gate

The image for commit `SHA` must not be published or move a mutable channel unless that same `SHA` has successful results for:

- `ci-required`
- `test-frontend`
- `check-generated-contracts`
- `migration-gate`

Requirements:

- verify the exact head SHA, not merely the latest successful workflow on `main`;
- fail closed when a required result is missing, cancelled, stale, or attached to another commit;
- preserve merge-queue/current-merge-candidate protection before merge and exact-commit verification after merge;
- do not use a mutable branch or tag as evidence of CI success;
- write the verified check/run identifiers and commit SHA to `GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY`;
- do not grant package-write permissions to code from an untrusted pull-request context.

## Architecture options

Choose one explicit orchestration model and document it:

### Preferred: main-CD orchestrator with reusable workflows

```text
main push
   ├─ required CI for exact SHA
   ├─ migration gate for exact SHA
   └─ after all required results succeed
          └─ reusable app-image build/publish workflow
```

This keeps ordering in one workflow graph and passes the immutable SHA directly to the build.

### Acceptable: post-CI workflow with exact-SHA verification

A `workflow_run` or dispatch-based publisher may be used only if it:

- is restricted to completed successful runs on `main`;
- obtains the triggering run's immutable head SHA;
- queries every other required result for that same SHA;
- rejects missing or non-success conclusions;
- checks out and builds that exact SHA;
- cannot be triggered into write-capable execution by untrusted PR code.

Do not rely on timing assumptions between independent push workflows.

## Implementation

### 1. Establish the exact-commit gate

Create or refactor the workflow topology so Docker build jobs cannot start until the required results above are successful for the same SHA.

The gate should emit a compact summary:

| Required result | SHA | Conclusion | Run/check |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ci-required` | `<sha>` | `success` | `<id/url>` |
| `test-frontend` | `<sha>` | `success` | `<id/url>` |
| `check-generated-contracts` | `<sha>` | `success` | `<id/url>` |
| `migration-gate` | `<sha>` | `success` | `<id/url>` |

### 2. Make the build consume the exact SHA

- Pass the approved commit SHA into checkout explicitly.
- Stamp the SHA and version into OCI metadata and the workflow summary.
- Preserve immutable digest publication before any mutable channel update.
- Ensure the multiarchitecture manifest is assembled only from digests produced for the approved SHA.

### 3. Remove duplicate frontend/contracts setup and commands

After the exact-commit gate is in place, remove from the app-image build workflow:

- Python setup used only for frontend/OpenAPI revalidation;
- system dependency installation used only for that revalidation;
- pip cache and Python project installation used only for that revalidation;
- Node setup used only for that revalidation;
- the `Check frontend and generated contracts` step;
- `npm ci`, typecheck, lint, frontend unit tests, and `contracts:check` outside the Docker build itself.

The Dockerfile should continue performing the production frontend build as part of image construction. This issue removes duplicate **validation**, not the actual assets required by the image.

### 4. Add an image-level smoke check

Keep or add a lightweight verification that tests the produced image rather than repeating source tests. It should verify at least:

- the image starts or its entrypoint responds to a bounded smoke command;
- the Vite manifest and referenced frontend assets exist in the image;
- the build ID and OCI revision match the approved SHA/version;
- required runtime binaries are present;
- the per-platform digest is non-empty before manifest assembly.

The smoke check must use the image that will be published, not a separately rebuilt image with different inputs.

### 5. Preserve separation from `dashboard-dist`

Do not reintroduce cross-workflow `dashboard-dist` consumption as part of this issue. The Dockerfile's internal frontend build remains the source of image assets.

Artifact reuse may be evaluated later only with exact-SHA provenance, manifest/hash verification, and a demonstrated build-time benefit.

### 6. Update workflow contract tests

Change `tests/unit/test_docker_publish_workflow.py` so it proves:

- image publication has an exact-SHA required-CI gate;
- the approved SHA is passed explicitly into checkout/build metadata;
- frontend source validation is absent from the Docker workflow;
- no `npm run ui:typecheck`, `ui:lint`, `ui:test`, or `contracts:check` command remains outside image construction;
- Python and Node setup used solely by the removed validation is absent;
- image smoke verification is present;
- digest-based multiarchitecture publication remains intact;
- package-write permissions are confined to trusted post-CI publication jobs.

## Expected files

- `.github/workflows/docker-publish.yml`
- potentially a new `.github/workflows/main-cd.yml`
- potentially reusable workflow definitions under `.github/workflows/`
- `tests/unit/test_docker_publish_workflow.py`
- any new workflow-gate helper and its tests
- release/deployment documentation describing exact-SHA gating

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] No app-image build or publication job starts until the same commit SHA has successful `ci-required`, `test-frontend`, `check-generated-contracts`, and `migration-gate` results.
- [ ] Missing, cancelled, stale, or mismatched-SHA results fail closed.
- [ ] The approved immutable SHA is passed explicitly into checkout and image metadata.
- [ ] Docker publishing no longer reruns frontend typecheck, lint, frontend unit tests, or generated-contract validation.
- [ ] Python and Node setup used only by those duplicate checks is removed from the workflow.
- [ ] The production frontend assets are still built inside the Docker image.
- [ ] The published image receives an image-level smoke verification.
- [ ] Multiarchitecture digest assembly and publication remain correct.
- [ ] Package-write permissions are used only in trusted exact-commit post-CI jobs.
- [ ] Workflow summaries record the approved SHA and required result evidence.
- [ ] Workflow contract tests enforce the gate and absence of duplicate validation.

## Non-goals

- Reusing the removed `dashboard-dist` artifact in Docker builds.
- Redesigning stable-channel promotion or the Codex conformance gate.
- Changing the application Dockerfile's production frontend build to a separately deployed frontend.
- Removing image-level verification in favor of trusting source tests alone.
